name: Animal Science Products, Inc. v. Hebei Welcome Pharmaceutical Co. Ltd.
question: May a court exercise independent review of an appearing foreign country’s explanation of its own law (as held by the US Courts of Appeals for the 5th, 6th, 7th, 11th, and DC Circuits), or must a court defer to a foreign government’s legal statement, based on principles of international comity, if that foreign government appears before the court (as held by the opinion below in accord with the US Court of Appeals for the 9th Circuit)?
description: A case in which the Court decided that a federal court determining foreign law pursuant to Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 44.1 should accord respectful consideration to a foreign government’s submission, but the court is not bound to accord conclusive effect to the foreign government’s statements.
